record.title: Per-Task .md mirror generator + render surface
record.description:
Generate human-readable .md mirrors of each Task in task-list.json (one file per Task at docs/reference/tasks/ID-N.md) so Liam can read individual Tasks without loading the whole JSON into context, and render those mirrors in a UI surface that supports edit-back (text + status). Source design at docs/research/per-task-file-mirror-design.md. Cross-project tooling constraint per ┬¦7 of the design doc ŌĆö generator + render surface must NOT live inside knowledge-hub/scripts/ or lib/ since this is workflow tooling, not a KH product. Likely target: ~/.claude/plugins/, ~/.claude/tools/, or a separate dev-tooling repo (decide in 20.4 TECH.md).

## Acceptance criteria

- Per-Task .md mirror file shape ratified (Q1-Q8 in design doc ┬¦3 answered by Liam).
- Rendering surface recommendation ratified (Plannotator fork vs kh-knowledge-platform legacy code vs new minimal UI, per 20.2 research).
- Generator script lives in cross-project location per ┬¦7.
- Mirror regeneration trigger ratified (manual / Stop hook / CI guard ŌĆö design doc ┬¦2.4 default is B+C).
- UI surface supports view + edit of Task / Subtask text + status, with write-back to task-list.json (canonical) not just the mirror.
- Cross-project location choice does not break the existing parseTaskListWithWarnings ingress (workflow-orchestration's only canonical read path).

subtasks.title: RESEARCH ŌĆö rendering surface options (Plannotator + kh-knowledge-platform legacy + new minimal)
subtasks.description: Catalogue rendering options for the per-Task .md mirrors. Liam wants view + EDIT (text + status), not just annotate.
subtasks.details:
Per per-task-file-mirror-design.md ┬¦8. Two known inputs:

1. **Plannotator** (Liam locally forked). Current capability: annotation on markdown files; takes filename as input. Gap: needs edit-in-place + structured field edit (status enum, priority enum). Research smallest fork delta to add edit mode.

2. **knowledge-hub-knowledge-platform** legacy work at /Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub-knowledge-platform/. Built migrations + code rendering state files (backlog, roadmap) to platform UI. Catalogue relevant files, assess reusability as cross-project tooling vs KH-coupled.

Deliverable: docs/research/per-task-render-surface.md covering both inputs + recommendation (build on Plannotator fork, build on legacy, or new minimal UI) + cross-project tooling location implications per ┬¦7.

subtasks.title: CLI + plugin packaging + browser-close + port retry + --check flag (Slice 5, tool repo)
subtasks.description: CLI binary bin/task-view.js + plugin manifest .claude-plugin/plugin.json + shared-server wiring + --check flag for CI usage + browser-close detection (30s idle) + port retry (5 attempts).
subtasks.details:
**Repo boundary: external `task-view` repo.**

**Implements TECH ┬¦6.1-┬¦6.7:**
- ┬¦6.1 CLI binary `bin/task-view.js`: per upstream Plannotator `bin/plannotator.js` pattern. Uses `parseArgs` from `node:util`. Positional path argument; flags: `--no-browser` (bool, do not auto-open browser; print URL only), `--port <N>` (string, override random port), `--check` (bool, run one-shot mirror-regen sanity pass + exit non-zero on drift; for CI use per inv 42). When no path positional ŌåÆ call `inferPathFromCwd()` per ┬¦2.3. Calls `startTaskViewServer({ path, port })` from `@task-view/server/ledger`. Calls `openBrowser(server.url)` unless `--no-browser`. Prints `Server ready at ${server.url} ŌĆö close the tab to exit`. Awaits `server.waitForExit()`.
- ┬¦6.2 plugin manifest `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`: per Plannotator upstream + Claude plugin convention. Schema: `{ name: "task-view", version, description, commands: [{ name: "/task-view", description, script: "node_modules/.bin/task-view" }] }`. Slash command invocation in any Claude Code session: `/task-view [optional-path-to-ledger.json]`. Launches server, opens browser, prints readiness, waits for browser close per inv 41.
- ┬¦6.3 shared server rationale: CLI and plugin both call `startTaskViewServer(...)`. Same Bun process shape. Same routes. Plugin manifest invokes CLI binary; CLI binary calls into `@task-view/server/ledger`. There's no separate "plugin server" ŌĆö the plugin is a thin manifest pointing at the binary.
- ┬¦6.4 `--check` flag for CI: `task-view --check <path>` runs one-shot: read canonical ŌåÆ detectSchema ŌåÆ generate mirrors idempotently ŌåÆ compare against existing mirrors on disk ŌåÆ exit 0 if no drift / non-zero with diff summary if drift. KH CI integration is 20.12 scope.
- ┬¦6.5 browser-close detection: server tracks last-request timestamp. If `now - last_request > 30 seconds` AND at least one request has been served (so slow browser start doesn't kill server pre-load), `process.exit(0)`. Threshold is a constant in `packages/server/ledger.ts`, NOT user-configurable per inv 50.
- ┬¦6.6 port retry policy: upstream `MAX_RETRIES = 5` carries forward unchanged. On port collision, retry with fresh random port up to 5 times, then exit with "could not bind" per inv 49.
- ┬¦6.7 $PATH install convention: documented in README.md. Routes: `bunx task-view` (one-shot, no install); `bun install -g task-view` (global to `~/.bun/install/global/...` + symlink); Claude plugin marketplace `claude plugins install task-view` (installs to `~/.claude/plugins/task-view/`). Per OQ-T1 ratified default, v0.1.0 ships as GitHub Release tarball: `bun install -g github:liam-jons/task-view` works against the tagged release URL. npm + marketplace are FU-2.

**PRODUCT invariants covered:** 41 (plugin slash command launches + opens browser + prints readiness + waits for close), 42 (CLI binary same + `--no-browser` / `--port` / `--check` flags), 43 (no-path invocation scans CWD per ┬¦2.3; numbered list / friendly miss), 49 (port retry 5x then "could not bind"), 50 (browser-close detection 30s idle + at-least-one-request gate).

**Estimated effort:** ~1-2h.

**Acceptance gate:** `tests/integration/dual-entry.test.ts` boots via CLI + via plugin invocation; asserts both serve identical routes; `tests/integration/cli-flags.test.ts` each flag behaves (--no-browser skips openBrowser; --port overrides; --check exits 0 on no-drift); `tests/integration/cwd-scan.test.ts` zero/one/multiple `document_name` JSON files in CWD; `tests/integration/port-retry.test.ts` occupy port + assert retry up to 5; `tests/integration/browser-close.test.ts` no requests for 30s after first request + at-least-one-request ŌåÆ server exits 0; `tests/integration/plugin-launch.test.ts` slash command launch flow.

**Acceptance test results ŌĆö all 6 PLAN ┬¦20.11 integration tests PASS:**
- `tests/integration/dual-entry.test.ts` (3) ŌĆö CLI direct + plugin-script-target both serve `/api/ledger` with structurally identical bodies.

- `tests/integration/port-retry.test.ts` (3) ŌĆö retry-and-bind verified; `MAX_PORT_RETRIES = 5` constant asserted.
- `tests/integration/browser-close.test.ts` (4) ŌĆö at-least-one-request gate + idle exit + continuous-activity refresh.
- `tests/integration/plugin-launch.test.ts` (10) ŌĆö manifest structure + Claude plugin convention fields + `/task-view` slash-command + bin reachability + `package.json` bin field.

**task-view test baseline:** 687 ŌåÆ 733 (+46 new tests; 0 regressions). Verified via `bun run test` in the task-view repo on commit `e996d7f`.

**S64 carryforwards folded in:**
- **Finding-4 (MarkdownBody CommonMark):** rewired in E1 ŌĆö `<pre>` placeholder replaced with full `parseMarkdownToBlocks` ŌåÆ `groupBlocks` ŌåÆ `BlockRenderer` pipeline. New tests verify CommonMark + GFM elements render correctly.
- **`columnsForColumnSet` sibling re-export (Checker S64 nit):** dropped in E2.

**Deviations from brief (with rationale):**
1. C+D commit-bundling: ledger.ts naturally co-owns the browser-close + port-retry logic. Splitting them into two commits would have meant either (a) landing a "C" that doesn't yet have retry (so the brief-specified `MAX_RETRIES = 5` constant lives orphaned) or (b) artificially churning the file twice. Combined commit `feat(20.11.cd): ...` keeps git history honest; 5 task-view commits total (C+D, A, B, E1, E2) satisfies the brief's "5+ commits in task-view" requirement.
2. `InlineMarkdown` re-introduction (E1 scope expansion): the brief said "replace `<pre>` block in `MarkdownBody` with the existing `parseMarkdownToBlocks` + `BlockRenderer` pipeline." But BlockRenderer's `InlineMarkdown` named import was unresolved ŌĆö the 20.6 fork strip removed the upstream annotation-coupled InlineMarkdown, and 20.9 deferred re-implementation. I re-introduced a minimal InlineMarkdown (~120 lines, bold/italic/code/link/anchor-link tokens) as a load-bearing prerequisite. A follow-up Task should re-author the full annotation surface, or the doc comment in InlineMarkdown.tsx should be updated to reflect the deliberate minimalism. 2. **`apps/server/index.ts` has no direct unit test** ŌĆö only the spawned-CLI integration tests cover `parseCliArgs` + `inferPathFromCwd` + `runRegenCheck`. The pure helpers could be exported and unit-tested for faster feedback; the integration tests are sufficient for acceptance but ~150ms per spawn is slow if these helpers churn. Backlog candidate.

**Implements TECH ┬¦3.5 + ┬¦6.4 KH-side + PRODUCT inv 5 + 40 initial mirror baseline:**

Step exits non-zero if mirrors drift from canonical ŌåÆ CI fails ŌåÆ developer regenerates mirrors locally. Install task-view in CI via `bun install -g github:liam-jons/task-view@v0.1.0` per OQ-T1.
